channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Shutdowns began as a way to enforce federal law. Now Trump is using it to take more power
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The government shutdown, already the second-longest in history, with no end in sight, is quickly becoming a way for President Donald Trump to exercise new command over the government. It wasn't always this way.
item.dc:creator: STEPHEN GROVES, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: AG James' 'horrible judgment' backing Mamdani puts New Yorkers in 'jeopardy': GOP challenger
item.description: GOP challenger Michael Henry slammed NY attorney general Letitia James over her endorsement of socialist mayoral candidate Zohran Mamdani in a Fox News Digital interview.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: America’s ‘BAT’ man unveils tech built to outsmart a Chinese first strike
item.description: Shield AI unveils X-BAT, an AI-powered fighter jet designed to counter China's anti-access strategy by operating without runways, GPS, or constant communication.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Early voting begins in New York City mayor's race and contest to pick New Jersey's next governor
item.description: Polling places open Saturday for in-person voting in two of the year’s most closely watched elections: The New York City mayor’s race and the contest to pick New Jersey’s next governor.
item.dc:creator: Philip Marcelo, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: The CEO of the Alamo's historic site has resigned after a top Texas Republican criticized her
item.description: The CEO of the nonprofit managing the Alamo resigned after a powerful Republican state official criticized her publicly, suggesting that her views aren't compatible with the history of the Texas shrine. Kate Rogers said in a statement Friday that she had resigned the day before, after Lt. Gov.
item.dc:creator: JOHN HANNA, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Florida lawmaker revives E-Verify bill for stronger enforcement
item.description: tate lawmakers kicked off last year with not one but three special sessions on illegal immigration. A key debate then was E-Verify, which has now made its return to the statehouse.
item.dc:creator: Mackenzie LaPorte, WFLA, NBC

item.title: US sanctions Colombian president and family over drug trafficking allegations
item.description: Treasury Department accuses Colombian President Petro of enabling cocaine production surge while he calls sanctions a "complete paradox" in response.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Bioasphalt could be the savior of soybean farmers
item.description: Soybean farmers caught in the middle of a trade war could find a new market for their crops in the form of bioasphalt.
item.dc:creator: Alex Caprariello, NewsNation

item.title: DOJ prepares to send election monitors to California, New Jersey following requests from state GOPs
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — The Department of Justice is preparing to send federal election observers to California and New Jersey next month, targeting two Democratic states holding off-year elections following requests from state Republican parties.
item.dc:creator: JILL COLVIN and MICHAEL R. BLOOD, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Pentagon accepts $130 million donation to help pay the military during the government shutdown
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Pentagon confirmed Friday that it has accepted an anonymous $130 million gift to help pay members of the military during the government shutdown, raising ethical questions after President Donald Trump had announced that a friend had offered the gift to defray any shortfalls.
item.dc:creator: KONSTANTIN TOROPIN and LISA MASCARO, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: DOJ sends federal election watchers to California and New Jersey counties amid Republican requests
item.description: Democrats clash with Trump's DOJ over federal election observers in California and New Jersey, calling deployment an 'intimidation tactic.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Pentagon sends largest aircraft carrier to Caribbean amid boat strikes
item.description: Hegseth’s order comes as the Trump administration has been striking alleged drug-smuggling boats in the Caribbean Sea.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, NewsNation

item.title: Jeffries endorses Mamdani in New York City mayor's race
item.description: Just hours before the start of New York’s early voting, Jeffries broke that silence by endorsing the controversial candidate.
item.dc:creator: Mike Lillis, NewsNation

item.title: New Jersey early voting 2025: When locations open and how to find them
item.description: NEW JERSEY (PIX11) -- While Election Day is fast approaching, New Jersey residents will be able to cast their ballot as early as this weekend. In 2021, New Jersey established in-person early voting for registered voters at General Election polling locations.
item.dc:creator: Ben Mitchell, PIX 11

item.title: Inflation data unlikely to be released next month, White House says
item.description: The White House says there probably won't be an October inflation report due to the government shutdown.
item.dc:creator: Andrew Dorn, NewsNation

item.title: Transportation chief warns of travel impacts amid shutdown
item.description: Transportation Secretary Sean Duffy has said the federal safety system depends on air traffic controllers working without pay.
item.dc:creator: Meg Hilling, NewsNation

item.title: The Latest: Trump to meet with Asian leaders
item.description: President Donald Trump is going to Japan and South Korea next week to promote an epic financial windfall — at least $900 billion in investments for U.S. factories, a natural gas pipeline and other projects.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: New Jersey online portal helps residents claim lost funds
item.description: NEW JERSEY (PIX11) – New Jerseyans, did you know that you can check if you have unclaimed money in the Garden State? The New Jersey Unclaimed Property Administration has an online portal that residents can utilize to search for property they may be owed.
item.dc:creator: Erin Pflaumer, PIX 11

item.title: Live: Lawmakers hear from witnesses on Trump actions in Chicago
item.description: Witnesses are expected to testify to their personal experiences, trauma, alleged constitutional violations and labor impacts.
item.dc:creator: Steph Whiteside, NewsNation

item.title: Trump gives disaster declarations to Alaska and others but denies Illinois, Vermont and Maryland
item.description: President Donald Trump approved major disaster declarations for Alaska, Nebraska, North Dakota and the Leech Lake Band of Ojibwe late Wednesday, while denying requests from Vermont, Illinois and Maryland and leaving other states still waiting for answers.
item.dc:creator: GABRIELA AOUN ANGUEIRA, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Ex-Anoka Co. sheriff tapped as Minnesota U.S. marshal
item.description: James Stuart was one of two candidates for the job recommended earlier this year by Minnesota’s Republican congressional delegation. The other was former Minneapolis police union leader Bob Kroll.
item.dc:creator: MPR News Staff, MPR News

item.title: GOP senator blocks Democratic bill to pay all federal employees during shutdown
item.description: Sen. Ron Johnson (R-Wis.) objected to a request by Sen. Chris Van Hollen (D-Md.) on Thursday for the Senate to immediately consider and pass a bill to pay all federal workers, members of the military and federal contractors during the 2025 government shutdown.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, NewsNation

item.title: NYC Mayor Eric Adams endorses Andrew Cuomo
item.description: NEW YORK CITY (PIX11) — New York City Mayor Eric Adams endorsed independent candidate former New York Gov. Andrew Cuomo as his successor on Thursday. During Wednesday night’s final mayoral debate, Cuomo was the only candidate who said he’d accept an endorsement from Adams when asked by moderators.
item.dc:creator: Video credit: Henry Rosoff, NewsNation

item.title: 5 takeaways from the final New York City mayor's debate
item.description: There was no shortage of fireworks between Democrat Zohran Mamdani, independent Andrew Cuomo and Republican Curtis Sliwa.
item.dc:creator: Julia Manchester, NewsNation

item.title: George Santos says he's 'looking into' repaying fraud victims
item.description: Santos was ordered to pay more than $370,000 in restitution, but the president's clemency order wiped the debt clean.
item.dc:creator: Chris Cuomo, NewsNation

item.title: Antifa infiltrating Seattle homeless services, report finds
item.description: NewsNation found evidence of activists confronting volunteers and disrupting encampment cleanup efforts.
item.dc:creator: Rich McHugh, NewsNation

item.title: Preservation group urges pause in White House East Wing demolition
item.description: The destruction of a portion of the White House’s East Wing to create the ballroom has hit a nerve among critics.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, NewsNation

item.title: Florida bill seeks to label political offenses as hate crimes
item.description: TALLAHASSEE, Fla. (WFLA) - In the wake of recent politically motivated attacks across the nation, one state lawmaker is looking to change Florida statute. A new bill at the capitol is looking to expand Florida's hate-crime statute, shifting how hate crimes are defined and punished.
item.dc:creator: Mackenzie LaPorte, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Minnesota cities can't regulate guns. St. Paul is introducing an assault weapon ban anyway
item.description: St. Paul city leaders are expected introduce a gun ordinance Wednesday, as part of an ongoing push from several Minnesota cities to get the state to repeal a law that prevents stricter municipal gun laws.
item.dc:creator: MPR News Staff, MPR News

item.title: Behind closed doors, Illinois lawmakers discuss potential redistricting effort
item.description: CHICAGO — As the redistricting battle plays out across the county, political leaders in Illinois are staying quiet about their plans to redraw congressional maps here. But under intense pressure from democrats in Washington D.C., the debate is starting to spill out into public view.
item.dc:creator: Tahman Bradley, WGN 9

item.title: DeSantis joins Democrat in nationwide push for term limits in Congress
item.description: TAMPA, Fla. (WFLA) — Governor Ron DeSantis is teaming up with a former Democratic representative to make a push for term limits in Congress. On Tuesday, the governor announced he and former Rep. David Trone of Maryland would co-chair U.S.
item.dc:creator: Sophia Fanning, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Anti-science bills hit statehouses, stripping away public health protections built over a century
item.description: More than 420 anti-science bills attacking longstanding public health protections – vaccines, milk safety and fluoride – have been introduced in statehouses across the U.S. this year.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Government shutdown expected to delay SNAP, CalFresh benefits
item.description: As the government marks 21 days into its shutdown, officials are stating that CalFresh and Supplemental Nutrition Assistance Program (SNAP) benefits may be delayed for millions of Californians.
item.dc:creator: Chris Ponce, Fox 5

item.title: Senate Republicans go to White House in show of unity
item.description: As the government shutdown enters its fourth week, Senate Republicans are headed to the White House — not for urgent talks on how to end it, but for a display of unity with President Donald Trump as they refuse to negotiate on any Democratic demands.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Californians have until midnight to register to vote in Nov. 4 special election
item.description: Californians have until midnight tonight to register to vote in the statewide special election on Nov.
item.dc:creator: Iman Palm, Fox 5

item.title: China hits the U.S. back in trade war
item.description: China has expanded its export rules on rare earths, requiring foreign firms to get approval for exporting products containing even small amounts of China-originated materials.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: As deadline for Trump’s colleges compact looms, schools signal dissent
item.description: Of the original nine schools that received the Trump administration's Compact for Academic Excellence in Higher Education, the majority have indicated they are not planning on signing.
item.dc:creator: Elissa Nadworny, MPR News
